title: phalangium laxiflorum
side_effects: Phalangium laxiflorum can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing severe skin irritation, allergic reactions, and digestive issues. Ingesting even small amounts of the plant's leaves or roots can lead to symptoms like nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can cause skin rashes, blisters, and eczema. If not handled correctly, the plant's toxic compounds can also exacerbate existing health conditions, such as asthma or kidney disease.
